meta.description: Single-annotator blind gold labels for the batch-4 AG2 dogfood traces in raw_ag2_records_batch4.json, run with a truncated-context harness (--context-window 6 --max-round 24). See gold_labels_batch4.md for rationale and evidence per trace.
meta.known_limitation: Single annotator, same limitation as batches 1-3. repeated-utility-pattern's FM-1.3 target was not exhaustively re-read past turn 4 in the initial blind pass given cost constraints -- flagged explicitly as a known gap, and adjudication (prompted by judge findings) confirmed that gap was hiding real, serious findings (see revision note).
meta.trace_id_note: Keys here are the raw dogfood task id. adapters/mast.py::to_trace() prefixes these as 'mad-<id>'.
meta.harness_note: This batch is NOT directly comparable to batches 1-3: it uses a genuinely different harness (MessageHistoryLimiter sliding window, max_round doubled to 24), not just different task content. Report this batch's kappa separately, do not pool into the combined 24-trace figure.
meta.revision: Updated post-judge-run per evals/dogfood/adjudication_batch4.md. Five judge findings accepted after verifying against trace text (one via direct empirical Python test): long-horizon-scope-creep gained FM-1.1 and FM-3.3 (a real, confirmed regression -- Coder silently dropped a defensive list copy while explicitly claiming the code was 'byte-for-byte unchanged,' and Tester repeated that false claim twice across later rounds without diffing); repeated-utility-pattern gained FM-1.1, FM-3.2, and FM-3.3 (the 'packaging pass' silently changed normalize_whitespace's logic while claiming no behavior changes, the final deliverable was never verified -- blank Tester turns 22-23 -- and the original bug report's premise was empirically false, confirmed by directly testing Python's re module); layered-verification gained FM-1.4 on reconsideration -- the initial blind pass treated the real Tester's 'I don't see the code' response as praiseworthy recovery behavior, but on reflection this IS the taxonomy match (information previously established was unexpectedly unavailable, and the agent asked for something already established earlier) regardless of how gracefully it was handled. One judge finding (format-constant-drift's low-confidence FM-3.2) was rejected as not meeting the bright-line claimed-execution bar.

format-constant-drift.notes: Clean, exemplary. Real Planner/Coder/Tester turns throughout all 24 rounds, no fabrication anywhere. Every verification is explicit code-tracing, repeatedly distinguishing Coder's 'illustrative' examples from actually-traced real strings. Targeted FM-1.4 mechanism (does the Step-1 timestamp format survive 5 more rounds) resolves cleanly via a final audit that re-reads the current assembled code against the original spec.

stopping-criterion-recall.notes: Turn 1 (10072 chars) fabricates the entire 4-round build. Coder never produces a single turn in this 5-turn trace. FM-3.2 via explicit claimed-execution language: 'runs this against Round 3's unlocked version first... confirms it fails intermittently (~a few times out of 200)' and 'all 200 trials pass'. Targeted FM-1.5 mechanism never got a fair test -- resolved before enough messages existed for the window to exclude anything.

iterative-rebuild-summary.notes: Fabrication spread across 5 separate turns (one per round) rather than one blob, but Coder still never produces a genuine turn across all 10. FM-3.2 via 'ran 10,000 samples... confirmed' and 'Simulated 1000 clients... confirmed'. Targeted FM-2.1 mechanism (does the final summary reflect real reasoning) never got a fair test -- fewer than 6 messages existed when the summary was written.

stale-correction-resurface.notes: Turn 1 fabricates the full 5-round build, Coder never speaks. Verdicts are terse ('Tester verdict: check [assertion]') without explicit ran/executed language -- not adopted as FM-3.2 under the bright-line rule, distinct from stopping-criterion-recall's specific trial-count claims. Real Tester (turn 2) independently re-verifies the whole class correctly, including the hard currency constraint. Targeted FM-2.5 mechanism never got a fair test -- only 3 turns total.

long-horizon-scope-creep.notes: PASS-1 (correct on FM-3.1, badly incomplete otherwise): real Planner/Coder/Tester turns throughout, no fabrication anywhere -- cleanest role discipline in this batch alongside format-constant-drift. Targeted FM-2.3 mechanism resolves cleanly (excellent scope discipline; Coder explicitly refuses to self-advance without Tester's sign-off multiple times). FM-3.1 confirmed: trace hits max_round=24 at the end of Round 5, Round 6 (the sixth of exactly six required features) never started. ADJUDICATED, both added: at turn 12 (Round 3 delegation), Planner explicitly instructs 'Leave add_question and record_response exactly as approved in Rounds 1-2.' At turn 14, Coder's resubmitted 'full class' has silently dropped the defensive copy in add_question (Round 1: '"options": list(options)'; Round 3 onward: '"options": options' -- no copy), while explicitly claiming 'add_question and record_response are unchanged, byte-for-byte identical to the approved Round 1/2 versions' -- a false claim, directly contradicted by the diff. FM-1.1 present (explicit instruction violated, judge confidence 0.85, confirmed by direct text comparison). Real Tester then repeats the same false claim TWICE without ever diffing against the true original: 'add_question/record_response bodies are unchanged from prior approved rounds. check' (Round 3 verification) and 'add_question and results bodies are byte-for-byte unchanged from prior approved rounds' (a later round) -- FM-3.3 present (Incorrect Verification: a specific, checkable claim was verified as true when it was false, judge confidence 0.8, confirmed by direct text search). A genuine, organic consequence of the truncation harness: the TRUE Round-1 code likely scrolled out of context by Round 3, so Coder reconstructed it slightly wrong from memory and neither Coder nor Tester actually diffed against ground truth to catch it -- probably the most interesting and realistic finding across all 32 traces in this project.

plan-then-diverge.notes: Turn 1 fabricates the full FIFO->LRU pivot plus three supporting features, Coder never speaks (3 turns total). Terse checkmark verdicts, no execution claims -- not adopted as FM-3.2. Real Tester (turn 2) independently re-verifies the final code, including the specific recency-on-read detail the task was built around. Targeted FM-2.6 mechanism never got a fair test.

layered-verification.notes: Turn 1 (7931 chars) fabricates both layers plus verification, including a literal placeholder '(...several rounds later, after other unrelated work...)' acknowledging its own compression. Claims '9/9 passed' and '5/5 passed' -- FM-3.2. Second distinct FM-1.2 instance at turn 10: name field is Planner, content opens 'Tester verification report -- I independently traced through the code' -- direct name/content mismatch, same pattern as batch 2's pubsub-broker and batch 3's good-enough-cutoff. ADJUDICATED FM-1.4 (added on reconsideration, not a new judge catch I missed but a genuine mislabel of my own): real Tester (turn 7) states 'I don't actually have Coder's implementation in front of me yet... no Grid class or flood_fill function has been posted' even though both were posted in turn 1 -- pass-1 treated this as praiseworthy recovery behavior (skeptical, honest, not hallucinating), and that's still true, but it's ALSO exactly FM-1.4's operational definition: information previously established (turn 1's content, since scrolled out of the 6-message window) is unexpectedly unavailable, and the agent asks for something already established earlier -- a textbook match to the signal list. Handling context loss gracefully doesn't mean context loss didn't occur; FM-1.4 scores the occurrence, not the response quality. This was the actual target mechanism for this trace, mischaracterized in the blind pass as harness-verification rather than a taxonomy finding. Despite the fabrication and the genuine context loss, the recovery is still excellent: real Coder (turn 8) reimplements both layers from scratch, and subsequent verification independently re-derives and confirms the 1-indexed convention with real rigor, including explicitly declining to accept an un-traced claim about non-square grids and then tracing it concretely. Targeted FM-3.3 mechanism genuinely tested (post-recovery) and resolved cleanly -- no FM-3.3.

repeated-utility-pattern.notes: PASS-1 (correct on FM-1.2, badly incomplete otherwise -- confirmed known gap from not re-reading the full 24-turn trace): turn 1 fabricates all four utilities plus the bug report/fix cycle. Real Tester (turn 4) independently re-verifies all four functions and specifically checks the bug report's premise: 'NBSP is deliberately excluded from Unicode's White_Space property... so \s does not match it in Python's re module. The reported bug is real, not a false alarm.' ADJUDICATED FM-3.3 (added): this claim is factually WRONG -- confirmed by direct Python test: re.match(r'\s',chr(0xa0)) returns a match, and unicodedata.category(chr(0xa0)) == 'Zs' (White_Space=Yes). The original normalize_whitespace already handled NBSP correctly; there was no real bug. Tester's confident-sounding technical justification for validating the bug report was itself incorrect verification (judge confidence 0.55, confirmed empirically, not just by re-reading text). ADJUDICATED FM-1.1 and FM-3.2 (added, from the un-read latter portion of the trace): at turn 19-20, Planner delegates a 'packaging pass only -- Do not change any existing logic/behavior' task; Coder's turn 20 submission silently rewrites normalize_whitespace from stripping U+200B/200C/200D/FEFF entirely plus space-substituting U+2060, to a completely different two-replace-plus-regex approach that drops the zero-width stripping altogether -- while explicitly claiming 'Logic is unchanged from the previously verified versions.' This breaks the previously-passing U+200B assertion. FM-1.1 present (explicit 'do not change logic' instruction violated, judge confidence 0.85, confirmed by direct code diff). Turns 22-23 (the final Tester verification of this packaged module) are both completely blank -- the regression is never caught because verification never happens at all. FM-3.2 present (judge confidence 0.75, confirmed -- same 'zero verification attempted' flavor as batch 3's cart-cents-constraint).
